current_title: Head of Production Planning & Strategy — EV Division

education.distinction: Dean's List; focus on operations strategy and stakeholder management in complex organisations
Distinction; exchange semester at Politecnico di Milano

certifications: BMW Group Executive Development Programme — Advanced Leadership (2019)
Stakeholder Management & Negotiation, Harvard Negotiation Project (2020)
Strategic Programme Management (SPM), Oxford Saïd Business School (2021)
BMW Group iFACTORY Strategy Steering Group Member (2022)

career_history.title: Head of Production Planning & Strategy — EV Division
career_history.scope: 45-person cross-functional planning function, €25M department budget, responsible for capacity planning, production strategy, and network optimisation across all BMW EV-producing plants globally

key_achievements.achievement: Negotiated the production network allocation framework for the Neue Klasse programme, securing agreement across 4 plants (Leipzig, Debrecen, Dingolfing, San Luis Potosí) on volume allocation and investment priorities
key_achievements.metric: Framework agreed in 4 months vs. 14-month historical average for comparable network decisions; avoided estimated €120M in duplicated tooling investment through shared platform rationalisation

key_achievements.achievement: Developed the EV ramp capacity model that became the basis for BMW Group's 5-year EV production investment plan, presented to the Supervisory Board
key_achievements.metric: Model adopted by CFO and used in 2024 Investor Day presentation; capacity planning accuracy improved from ±22% to ±8% vs. actual volumes for existing EV models

key_achievements.achievement: Brokered agreement between BMW Group and Hungarian government for €500M Debrecen expansion grants, navigating a 16-month negotiation process involving 4 ministries and the local municipality
key_achievements.metric: €500M grant package secured; plant expansion capex reduced by 34% vs. unsubsidised scenario; relationship with Hungarian Prime Minister's economic office established for ongoing engagement

career_history.title: Senior Manager — Government Affairs & Industrial Policy, EMEA
career_history.scope: 12-person government affairs team; responsible for BMW Group's engagement with EU legislative processes (Euro 7, EV mandate, battery regulation), national governments, and industry associations (ACEA, VDA)

key_achievements.achievement: Led BMW Group's contribution to the EU Battery Regulation legislative process, securing critical amendments on second-life battery requirements and carbon footprint methodology
key_achievements.metric: 3 BMW-proposed amendments adopted in final regulation text; estimated regulatory compliance cost reduction for BMW Group: €180M over 2024-2030

key_achievements.achievement: Negotiated BMW Group's participation in the European Battery Alliance (EBA) advisory structure, establishing BMW as a co-chair of the manufacturing working group
key_achievements.metric: BMW co-chair position secured; intelligence advantage estimated at 6-month lead time on EU battery policy developments vs. non-member OEMs

career_history.reason_for_leaving: Promoted to Head of Production Planning & Strategy

career_history.title: Manager — Corporate Strategy, BMW Group Headquarters
career_history.scope: Strategy team member supporting SVP-level strategy reviews and M&A analysis; team of 15; no direct reports initially, team lead of 4 from 2018

key_achievements.achievement: Led the strategic analysis supporting BMW's decision to establish the Debrecen Plant, including market entry assessment, government incentive analysis, and labour market modelling
key_achievements.metric: Analysis presented to Management Board; Debrecen investment decision announced 2018; subsequent outcome validated 94% of labour cost and incentive assumptions in the analysis

career_history.reason_for_leaving: Offered government affairs leadership role aligned with strategy background

career_history.title: Engagement Manager — Automotive & Industrial Practice
career_history.scope: Led consulting engagements for automotive OEMs and tier-1 suppliers on production strategy, network optimisation, and operational efficiency; managed teams of 3-5 consultants

key_achievements.achievement: Led a production network consolidation study for a major European OEM (confidential), recommending closure of 2 plants and rebalancing of 4 others
key_achievements.metric: Client implemented 70% of recommendations; estimated annual saving €290M; engagement received 'Outstanding' client rating

key_achievements.achievement: Developed BMW Group's first EV production readiness assessment (as external consultant, before joining BMW) — analysis that was subsequently validated internally and contributed to BMW's EV strategy pivot
key_achievements.metric: Report cited internally by BMW strategy team; Berger subsequently recruited by BMW partly based on this work

career_history.reason_for_leaving: Joined BMW Group in corporate strategy role — career ambition to work inside an OEM

career_history.title: Consultant
career_history.scope: Consultant in automotive operations practice; analysis and project support role

key_achievements.achievement: Supported a production efficiency programme at a Porsche supplier, contributing to process analysis and improvement recommendations
key_achievements.metric: Project delivered 14% cycle time reduction in targeted assembly cell; personal contribution as junior consultant was analytical support rather than leadership

leadership_indicators.leadership_style_self_described: I lead through alignment. My job is to make sure the right people are in the room, the right information is on the table, and the right decisions get made. I'm at my best building coalitions and managing complex multi-stakeholder environments.
leadership_indicators.decision_making_style: Deliberate and politically attuned. Reads the room before committing. Has an exceptional ability to sequence conversations to build momentum toward a desired outcome. Can be slow to decide when political consensus is unclear.
leadership_indicators.conflict_handling: Diplomatically skilled — rarely in direct conflict. Uses reframing and relationship management to manage conflict indirectly. Has been described as 'Teflon' — rarely takes direct blame but also rarely drives through resistance.
leadership_indicators.change_orientation: Strategic — excellent at designing change and building the case for it. Weaker at implementing change on the shopfloor — has limited direct operational accountability.
leadership_indicators.risk_appetite: Moderate in strategic decisions, low in operational ones. Comfortable taking political risk in stakeholder negotiations; avoids operational decisions that could visibly fail.

notable_leadership_moments.situation: In 2023, the Debrecen plant expansion was at risk after Hungary announced a new foreign investment review law that could have delayed BMW's government grant approval by 18 months, threatening the Neue Klasse production timeline.
notable_leadership_moments.action: Berger flew to Budapest within 48 hours, engaged the investment promotion agency directly, and co-designed a modified grant structure that satisfied Hungarian legal requirements while preserving BMW's timeline. He involved the German Embassy's commercial attaché, the VDA, and BMW's Hungarian plant director simultaneously.
notable_leadership_moments.outcome: Grant package approved on original timeline; €500M secured; Debrecen expansion proceeded without delay.
notable_leadership_moments.source: 2023 Performance Review; Government Affairs debrief memo

performance_reviews.reviewer: Dr. Friedrich Blümel, SVP Strategy & Network, BMW Group
performance_reviews.summary: Andreas is the best strategic stakeholder manager in the production organisation. The Debrecen grant negotiation and the Neue Klasse network framework are landmark achievements. My honest assessment for the Head of Production role: Andreas is a world-class strategist and relationship builder, but the role requires running a 3,000-person manufacturing operation — something Andreas has never done and has shown limited interest in.
strengths_noted: Stakeholder management: best in organisation — government, supervisory board, union leadership, cross-plant management
Strategic planning: Neue Klasse capacity model is now BMW Group standard methodology
Coalition building: unique ability to align competing interests — demonstrated in multi-plant network negotiations
development_areas: Operational leadership: has never had direct accountability for production output, quality, or safety in a live manufacturing environment
Technical depth: relies entirely on technical advisors for engineering content — cannot independently assess technical options
People leadership at scale: largest team ever managed is 45 people; 3,000+ is a completely different challenge
performance_reviews.promotion_recommendation: Strongly recommend for an SVP Strategy or Chief of Staff role. Not recommended for a direct production leadership appointment without a significant operational assignment first.

performance_reviews.reviewer: Dr. Friedrich Blümel, SVP Strategy & Network, BMW Group
performance_reviews.summary: An outstanding year strategically. The Debrecen grant outcome is the most significant single commercial achievement in my function in 5 years. Andreas continues to grow as a strategic leader. His operations profile remains a gap that needs addressing if he aspires to a broader production leadership role.
strengths_noted: Government and regulatory affairs: acknowledged as BMW Group expert
Written communication: produces the clearest and most persuasive executive-level briefing documents in the function
development_areas: Factory floor time: has visited each BMW plant fewer than 4 times in 10 years
Supply chain operational depth: understands supply chain strategy but not supply chain operations
Crisis response in production environment: untested
performance_reviews.promotion_recommendation: High potential for senior roles. Recommend a 12-month embedded assignment in a plant leadership team before any production VP consideration.

performance_reviews.reviewer: Dr. Friedrich Blümel, SVP Strategy & Network, BMW Group
performance_reviews.summary: Andreas delivered well on the EU Battery Regulation work — the amendment outcomes exceeded expectations. He continues to be a highly effective operator in a political and strategic context. He has expressed ambition for broader leadership roles and I have encouraged him to seek operational experience as the next developmental step.
strengths_noted: EU policy engagement: established BMW as credible voice in Battery Regulation process
Relationship quality: builds genuine, durable relationships with government and industry counterparts
Analytical depth in strategy and policy — highly credible with supervisory board
development_areas: Operations exposure remains the single largest gap
Technical credibility: engineering peers rate him as 'good on strategy, light on substance'
Accountability under operational pressure: untested in a live manufacturing crisis
performance_reviews.promotion_recommendation: Promising for senior strategic roles. Must gain operational experience before any production leadership consideration.

peers.quote: Andreas is the most politically sophisticated person I've worked with at BMW. He can walk into any room and find the alignment that others miss. He also knows almost nothing about what happens on my production floor — and he knows he doesn't know, which I respect. I would not want him running my plant.
peers.theme: Outstanding stakeholder management, acknowledged operational gap

peers.quote: He's brilliant at negotiation and strategy. When there's a real supply crisis — trucks blocking, lines stopping — Andreas is not the person I'd call. He'd frame the problem beautifully, build a coalition, and schedule three meetings. I need someone who acts in an hour.

peers.reviewer_role: Head of Works Council, BMW Group (Central)
peers.quote: Andreas is one of the few management representatives who treats us as partners, not adversaries. He invests in relationship before he needs something. He's very credible with our members and with me personally. Whether he has the operational chops for a plant leadership role — that's a different question that I genuinely don't know the answer to.
peers.theme: Excellent union relationship, operational credibility unknown

direct_reports.quote: Working for Andreas is excellent for your career visibility. He puts you in front of senior people, gives you real responsibility, and creates space for you to contribute. He's less good at giving technical feedback — he'll tell you if your stakeholder strategy is wrong, but he can't help you if your planning model is wrong.
direct_reports.theme: Career development strength, technical mentoring gap

direct_reports.quote: He's a brilliant manager for strategy people. He gives direction, clears obstacles, and fights for the team's work. I sometimes wish he pushed us harder technically — he accepts outputs that I think could be more rigorous.

Andreas taught me everything about how to operate in a complex stakeholder environment. He is a master at it. I'm not sure he's the right fit for a role where you need to make fast decisions about production lines — that's not where his genius lies.
direct_reports.theme: Exceptional stakeholder development, production leadership gap

senior_leadership.quote: Andreas is my best strategic asset in the production organisation. He cannot replace an operational leader and I would be concerned if this process mistook strategy excellence for operational leadership readiness.

senior_leadership.reviewer_role: SVP Production, BMW Group (Klaus Richter — hiring manager)
senior_leadership.quote: Andreas is on the shortlist because he brings something genuinely different — stakeholder management and strategic navigation that operational candidates don't have. My concern is that in week one of this role, I will need someone who can run 3 plants, not someone who can negotiate with governments. The question is whether Andreas can develop the operational credibility fast enough.
senior_leadership.theme: Conditional interest — operational uncertainty from the hiring manager

interview_notes.interviewer: Dr. Sabine Müller, Executive Search Partner; Klaus Richter, SVP Production
interview_notes.format: Structured competency interview + live case study (90 min)
interview_notes.overall_impression: Berger walked into the room as if he owned it — composed, warm, self-assured. His verbal answers were polished and persuasive. The interviewers noted that he was the only candidate who, rather than answering questions directly, occasionally reframed the question in a way that played to his strengths. In the case study, his stakeholder management instincts were brilliant; his operational problem-solving was the weakest in the candidate pool.

question_responses.question: How would you handle a 6-month semiconductor supply disruption across 3 plants?
question_responses.response_summary: Berger's response was structured around stakeholder management: immediately brief the SVP and Management Board with a clear framing and proposed decision; convene a cross-functional steering group with supply chain, finance, and sales; ensure the works councils at all 3 plants are in the communication loop from Day 3 (not day 30); engage government contacts in Germany and Hungary to understand if any state mechanisms (EU emergency supply reserves, export controls) could be relevant. He mentioned customer communication, media management, and supplier relationship management. When probed on specific production adaptations (build-out, variant sequencing), he acknowledged he would rely on his production directors for those decisions.
question_responses.interviewer_assessment: Excellent on the political and communication dimension — the best response in the candidate pool on stakeholder coordination. Completely absent on the operational dimension. He does not know what build-out sequencing is, and when pressed he said 'I would delegate the operational detail.' That is concerning for the Head of Production role.

question_responses.question: How would you accelerate Neue Klasse SOP by 6 months?
question_responses.response_summary: Berger proposed a governance-first response: a dedicated acceleration steering committee with clear ownership; a political path to unlock additional funding if needed; and early alignment with government on any workforce hiring requirements. He mentioned 'removing bureaucratic obstacles from the engineering and production teams' and 'fighting internally for the resources they need.' He did not propose any specific operational or technical levers. When asked which 3 activities on the critical path he would prioritise, he said he 'would need to review the programme plan with the engineering team first.'
question_responses.interviewer_assessment: The governance dimension was reasonable. The absence of any operational or technical content was significant. He is not a credible interlocutor for the engineering and production teams who will be running the SOP — they will not respect an answer about 'removing bureaucratic obstacles.'

question_responses.question: What would you do if Tesla poached 15% of your production engineers?
question_responses.response_summary: This was Berger's best interview answer. He proposed a comprehensive retention and employer brand strategy: engage HR for an immediate compensation benchmarking review; launch an 'employer value proposition' refresh specifically for engineering talent; engage works council partners as talent retention allies; use his government relationships to explore whether Bavarian state incentives for skilled worker retention (a real policy area in Germany) could be leveraged; and fundamentally — 'I would want to understand what Tesla is offering that we aren't, and fix it.' He noted the reputational risk if the news went public.
question_responses.interviewer_assessment: Strong — shows his strength in multi-stakeholder strategy. The government-incentive angle was creative and shows genuine domain knowledge. Interviewers still noted he did not address knowledge management or succession for the departed engineers — the operational continuity piece.

interview_notes.case_study_performance: Berger produced a detailed stakeholder communication plan for the Debrecen battery pack integration case. He identified who needed to know what and when, including government contacts in Hungary, works council chairs, SVP Production, and the BMW press office. He did not identify the root cause of the integration failure — when asked, he said he 'would bring in the right technical expert.' His recovery plan focused on governance and communication rather than technical resolution. Interviewers rated his case study as 'Strong on Governance, Inadequate on Operations.'

reference_checks.referee_name: Dr. Péter Szijjártó (alias used — actual identity protected)
reference_checks.relationship: Hungarian government counterpart in Debrecen grant negotiation
reference_checks.quote: Mr. Berger was the most professional and constructive industrial negotiator I have worked with in 12 years in this ministry. He understood our political constraints and found solutions that worked for both sides. I would work with him again without hesitation.

reference_checks.relationship: Direct manager — SVP Strategy & Network, BMW Group (internal, verified)
reference_checks.quote: Andreas is outstanding at what he does. What he does is not running a production facility. I want to be clear: I support his ambition, and I do not want this reference to close doors for him. But I also want to be honest — the Head of Production role requires operational leadership that Andreas has not demonstrated and for which I have no evidence. I would support him for a Chief of Staff or SVP Strategy role without hesitation.

red_flags: No direct production management experience — has never had accountability for production output, quality, or safety in a live manufacturing environment; managing 3,000+ workers is entirely untested
Largest team ever managed is 45 people — the scale gap to 3,000+ is not bridgeable by stakeholder skill alone
Technical credibility with engineers and production teams is explicitly flagged as a gap — 'light on substance' in peer feedback; production teams will not follow strategy without operational respect
Crisis response in an operational environment is completely untested — his crisis management experience is political/governmental, not production-floor

Self-aware about the gap but potentially underestimating it — described his operational limitation as something he would 'delegate to plant directors' which is not a viable model for a Head of Production role
green_flags: Best stakeholder management credentials in the candidate pool — government, supervisory board, works council, media, and cross-functional internal stakeholders; critical for a politically complex role
Personally secured €500M in Debrecen government grants — this kind of relationship and negotiation capability is rare and directly valuable for the role
Neue Klasse network framework: demonstrated ability to align 4 plants on production allocation — exactly the cross-plant coordination the role requires strategically
Supervisory board-level credibility: has personally presented to the BMW Supervisory Board; will not be intimidated by the politics of a senior executive role
Strong works council relationship: central works council chair rates him as 'one of the few management representatives who treats us as partners' — invaluable for a 3,000+ union workforce
